explicit_text: My parents met in Japan, and my mom had her whole career set out there before we moved. Although we weren't supposed to leave, my dad couldn't find a job in Japan, which led us to move to the United States. My mom had to give up her dreams in Japan and took an under-the-table cash job here to support us.

explicit_text: My dad lived in Beijing before we moved to the United States. He was originally based there, while my mom lived in Shanghai. Their different locations made it challenging to balance family life before I was born.

explicit_text: My mom lived in Shanghai before we moved to the US, and I was fluent in Shanghainese as a toddler. I was raised by my maternal grandparents there until I was two, and I have memories of our home with chickens, a vegetable garden, and a shop. Moving away from Shanghai was hard because I haven't been back in over ten years.

explicit_text: My dad found a research position at the University of South Carolina, which is why we moved to the US. This opportunity was the turning point for our family, even though it meant leaving everything behind. We settled first in Columbia, South Carolina, near the university area.

explicit_text: We moved to the United States when I was very young because my dad got a research job here. It was a tough transition; we left behind all our family and had to start from scratch in a tiny apartment in Columbia, South Carolina. Now, years later, my family and I have become US citizens, and my little brother was even born here.

explicit_text: Our first home in the US was a tiny apartment in Columbia, South Carolina, where we had just one mattress on the floor. I was two years old and refused to go anywhere without my chicken toy, which my mom once had to walk all the way to my preschool to deliver. Life there was challenging as we adapted to a new language and culture.

explicit_text: After some time in Columbia, we moved to Charlotte, North Carolina, and have grown to love it here. It's been a fresh start for our family, and my little brother, born in the US, is now 12 years old. Charlotte feels like home now after all the changes we've been through.

explicit_text: When we moved to the US, I switched from speaking Shanghainese to Mandarin Chinese within two months, even though my dad spoke a different dialect. I didn't speak any English at first, but I quickly adapted by repeating everything my preschool teacher said. Learning Mandarin was part of how I adjusted to my new life here.

explicit_text: I still take all my photos on the same Canon digital camera that was used for all my baby photos. This camera has been with me through many stages of my life and holds a lot of sentimental value. It's a small but meaningful connection to my past and family memories.
